input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
## .NET MAUI Setup on macOS — Complete Guide

### 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Compilers, headers |
| **.NET SDK** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI |
| **MAUI workload** | Installed via `dotnet` CLI | MAUI templates & build targets |
| **Android SDK** | Bundled via MAUI workload or Android Studio | Android build tools, emulator |
| **JDK 17** | [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) or via MAUI workload | Android toolchain dependency |

---

### 2. Step-by-Step Installation

**A. Install Xcode**
```bash
# After installing from the App Store:
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
```

**B. Install .NET SDK**
Download the latest stable `.pkg` from [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download). Then verify:
```bash
dotnet --version
```

**C. Discover & install the MAUI workload**
```bash
# List available workloads to find the exact name/version:
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it:
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads:
dotnet workload list
```

**D. Android SDK + JDK (auto-provisioned)**
The MAUI workload installs the Android SDK and OpenJDK for you. To confirm paths:
```bash
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android \
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" \
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
```
Or install **Android Studio** manually if you want the emulator GUI manager.

---

### 3. Verification

```bash
# Check the full environment in one shot:
dotnet workload list
dotnet new maui -n HealthCheck
cd HealthCheck

# Build for both platforms:
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios        # requires Xcode
dotnet build -f net9.0-android    # requires Android SDK + JDK

# Run on iOS Simulator:
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# List available iOS simulators:
xcrun simctl list devices available
```

---

### 4. Optional but Recommended

| Tool | Why |
|------|-----|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) | Lightweight IDE with MAUI support |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (sunset) | No longer updated — use VS Code instead |
| **Android Studio** | Better emulator management, AVD creation |
| **Homebrew** (`brew install --cask dotnet-sdk`) | Alternative install path for .NET |

---

### 5. Key Troubleshooting Commands

```bash
dotnet workload repair          # fix broken workload installs
dotnet workload update          # update all workloads to latest
xcrun simctl list devices       # verify iOS simulators
adb devices                     # verify Android device/emulator
dotnet --info                   # full SDK + runtime diagnostic dump
```

### 6. Official Docs

- **Setup guide:** https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation
- **Supported versions matrix:** https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Release-Versions
- **Known issues:** https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Known-Issues

> **Version tip:** Always match the .NET SDK major version to the target framework (e.g., .NET 9 SDK → `net9.0-ios`). Run `dotnet workload search maui` to see which MAUI workload version pairs with your installed SDK.
